new_feedback.element: Empty state · right rail step counter vs guidance bar
new_feedback.issue: The guidance bar says "All three are required before an evaluation can start" and the form labels read step 1/2/3 of 3, but the disabled-Start caption says "4 steps remaining before start." Three required inputs plus the judge acknowledgment is four gates, yet the copy never reconciles the 3-vs-4 count. An examiner reading a control checklist will read the mismatch as the control inventory being uncertain about how many gates exist.
new_feedback.change: Make the counts agree. Either label the judge acknowledgment as the explicit fourth required gate in the guidance bar ("Four steps: target, spec, budget, judge acknowledgment") and renumber the form sections accordingly, or change the caption to "4 gates remaining: target, spec, budget, judge ack" so the number is itemized rather than a bare integer.

new_feedback.element: Cost meter · SESSION and EST/RUN chips (happy + error states)
new_feedback.issue: The EST/RUN face carries cost-model provenance ("cost-model v2.1 · 4c7b") but no "as of" capture time, and the SESSION chip ($61.40 / $250.00 mo) carries neither a timestamp nor a freshness marker on its face. A spend figure shown to a model risk officer without an as-of time is a distrust trigger: $61.40 could be live or an hour stale and nothing on the chip discloses which. The estimate/ledger drawer also opens with no capture timestamp on the figures.
new_feedback.change: Add a monospace "as of 14:07:5xZ" caption to both the EST/RUN and SESSION chip faces (and to the drawer header), reading the same clock the acknowledgment caption already uses, so every dollar figure on screen carries its capture instant the way the timestamps-next-to-metrics convention requires.

new_feedback.element: Live-updates pip · connected-state encoding across the three panels
new_feedback.issue: The same "Live updates · connected" status renders three different ways: happy and empty use a green pip with a glow ring, while the error panel uses a grey pip containing a tiny check, still labelled "connected." A reviewer comparing the three panels side by side cannot tell whether the grey-with-check error pip means a different transport state (degraded? reconnecting?) or is merely an inconsistent rendering of the identical "connected" value. State that looks different but reads the same label undermines the at-a-glance trust the status row is supposed to provide.
new_feedback.change: Render "connected" identically in all three states (one pip color + one glow treatment). If the error panel is meant to show a different transport condition, give it a distinct label (e.g. "reconnecting" / "degraded") rather than the same "connected" string under a different glyph.
